tool_input.summary: Identified Mars Exploration Rover (MER) Spirit-relevant PDS holdings that contain the key compositional/mineralogical (APXS, Mini-TES, Mössbauer) and imaging (Pancam) observations typically used to argue for co-located fumarolic (acid-sulfate, vapor-dominated) and hot-spring (silica-rich, aqueous) activity in the same volcanic-hydrothermal system (e.g., Columbia Hills/Home Plate region). Returned both PDS4 bundles/collections and the corresponding PDS3 volumes when present in the catalog search results.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Spirit (MER2) Mini-TES emissivity spectra are used to identify alteration/mineral assemblages (e.g., silica-rich materials vs sulfate-bearing coatings/soils) and to map their spatial association, a key line of evidence for both hot-spring sinter-like silica and fumarolic acid-sulfate alteration occurring within one hydrothermal setting.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MTES-4-EMR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart to Spirit Mini-TES derived emissivity products; supports reproducing the spectral identifications underlying the hydrothermal interpretation (silica vs sulfates) and their co-occurrence.

candidates.title: MER APXS Derived Oxide Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: APXS-derived oxide chemistry constrains element enrichments/depletions consistent with acid-sulfate fumarolic leaching (e.g., low mobile cations, S enrichment) and/or silica enrichment from hot-spring precipitation; can be compared across neighboring targets to argue they belong to one linked hydrothermal system.

candidates.title: MER1/
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 APXS oxide products including Spirit (MER2) allow direct replication of chemical trends used to infer hydrothermal processes (acid-sulfate vs silica-rich aqueous deposition) and evaluate whether they occur in spatial/stratigraphic proximity.

candidates.title: MER2 APXS Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: If you need to re-derive compositions or examine measurement context/quality, the Spirit APXS raw spectra support robust geochemical interpretation of leaching/precipitation processes tied to fumaroles and hot springs.

candidates.title: MER2 APXS Xray Spectra (Partially Processed) Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Intermediate APXS products useful for checking spectral fits and uncertainties behind oxide results used in hydrothermal system arguments.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MB-4-SUMSPEC-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Spirit Mössbauer summary spectra constrain Fe-mineralogy (e.g., hematite/goethite/jarosite-like phases vs basaltic minerals), helping distinguish fumarolic acid-sulfate oxidation from aqueous hot-spring alteration and showing both signatures in the same geologic setting.

candidates.title: MER2-M-PANCAM-2-EDR-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Spirit Pancam multispectral imaging provides geomorphic/stratigraphic context (layering, vent-related textures, bright-toned deposits, coatings) needed to argue fumarolic and hot-spring deposits are spatially associated within one volcanic hydrothermal system.

candidates.title: MER1: PANCAM OPERATIONS EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Not Spirit (MER1 is Opportunity), but included as a cautionary near-match from search results; use only if you are comparing rover cross-calibration or want the identical Pancam EDR structure. For Spirit imaging, prefer MER2 Pancam volumes/holdings.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
How do the Spirit observations support the idea that fumarolic activity and hot spring activity both occurred in the same volcanic hydrothermal system?
